lost foam casting of magnesium alloy:The foamed plastics is used to make a full mold pattern that is completely consistent with the structure and size of the product parts. After being dried with dipping coating fire-resistant coatings, the dry sand molding is carried out, and then the magnesium liquid is poured into the mold to make the pattern evaporate by heating, thus obtaining a precision casting method for magnesium alloy parts that are consistent with the shape and size of the mold. Compared with other foundry methods, it has the following characteristics: good surface quality of castings, high dimensional accuracy, high adaptability of casting structure design, good yield of thousand sand used for casting plastic, and can avoid the hot tendency of magnesium alloy during condensation shrinkage. The lost foam foundry process needs to be carried out under atmosphere protection. Lost foam casting (EPC) has been widely used in steel castings, but lost foam casting of magnesium alloy technology has developed slowly. In 2002, American ECK Corporation took the lead in successfully casting AZ91 magnesium alloy castings in lost foam casting. Although many advances have been made in the research of lost foam casting of magnesium alloy technology in China, such as new technologies such as magnesium alloy vacuum low-pressure lost foam foundry, and a variety of magnesium alloy castings such as motor shells, aircraft guide wheels, and exhaust pipes have been prepared, they are still in the experimental stage on the whole, and have not been applied in industry on a large scale.

magnesium alloy anodizing:Anodizing refers to the surface treatment technology of forming protective oxide film by taking magnesium alloy specimen as anode under the action of external electric station or current through the electrochemical reaction between specimen and treatment solution. According to the size of the applied charge, anodizing can be divided into two types: conventional anodizing and plasma micro arc oxidation. Conventional anodizing is carried out under low voltage. Dow17 and HAE are the most representative magnesium alloy anodizing processes. The magnesium alloy anodic oxide film has the advantages of strong adhesion with the matrix metal, good electrical insulation, corrosion resistance and wear resistance. At the same time, its porous structure can be used as the coating substrate to improve the adhesion between the coating and the alloy matrix. Magnesium alloy micro arc oxidation is carried out under high pressure (usually 200-600V), and its research and use are more extensive than conventional anodic oxidation. Compared with chemical conversion coating, magnesium alloy anodic oxide coating has better corrosion resistance and wear resistance, but its cost is slightly higher and the process is more complex.

magnesium alloy hollow thinwalled profile:Usually refers to magnesium alloy profiles with multiple closed through-holes and smaller wall thickness in the cross-section. Due to the complex structure of magnesium alloy hollow thinwalled profile, AZ31 magnesium alloy with good plasticity is often used for preparation at present. The magnesium alloy hollow thinwalled profile is mainly obtained by extrusion method of shunt combined die. During extrusion, the solid magnesium alloy ingot is first divided into multiple streams of metal flow by the shunt die, and then the metal flow converges in the welding chamber. After welding under high temperature and high pressure, it is extrusion out of the mold hole for forming. When extrusion magnesium alloy hollow thinwalled profile, it is often necessary to adjust the die structure to make the outflow speed of each metal stream uniform, otherwise it is easy to cause warpage, fracture of the profile, or even can not fill the die hole. The straightening of magnesium alloy hollow thinwalled profile is still a technical difficulty because of their complex structure and multiple closed through holes. At present, the main application prospect of magnesium alloy hollow thinwalled profile is the overall shape structure of high-speed trains, subway trains, light rail trains, double deck passenger cars, luxury buses, freight cars, special vehicles and other vehicles.

self sealing hole micro arc oxidation of magnesium alloy:The micro arc oxidation film is a porous ceramics structure, which usually requires post sealing treatment to reduce porosity and improve corrosion resistance. Self sealing micro arc oxidation film refers to the automatic sealing of micropores in the film by oxides during the film forming process, without the need for post sealing treatment. At present, magnesium alloy self sealing micro arc oxidation films have been obtained in electrolytes containing fluorotitanate and fluorozirconate. In addition to magnesium oxide, the film layer components also contain a large amount of titanium oxide and zirconium oxide with higher chemical stability. The corrosion resistance is 5-6 times higher than that of ordinary micro arc oxidation films, and the surface does not need to be coated with organic coatings to provide good protection for the magnesium substrate.

magnesium matrix composite:The composite materials reinforcement materials made from magnesium or magnesium alloy as the matrix and compounded with various high-performance reinforcement materials materials can be fibrous, granular and crystal whisker carbide, boride, oxide and graphite (carbon), with the size usually ranging from nanometer to micrometer, and the content generally ranging from 5% to 75%. Magnesium matrix composite not only maintain the characteristics of magnesium alloys, but also have the comprehensive characteristics of composite materials. By optimizing the combination of magnesium alloy and different reinforcements, magnesium matrix composite with special properties and excellent comprehensive properties can be obtained. The main manufacturing methods include powder metallurgy, melt infiltration, stirring casting method and spray codeposition.

Mg based biomaterials:The biomaterials based on magnesium or magnesium alloys skillfully make use of the characteristics of magnesium alloys that are prone to corrosion (degradation) in the human environment to achieve the medical and clinical purpose of metal implant gradually degradation in the body until the final disappearance. Mg based biomaterials include pure magnesium, biomedical magnesium alloy, biomedical magnesium matrix composite, etc. Mg based biomaterials play the unique advantages of magnesium as a biological materials: it has excellent mechanical properties, and its elastic modulus, compressive yield strength and density are closer to human bone than other metal based biological materials; At the same time, it has the advantages of degradation, harmless degradation products to human body, and good biocompatibility. The current research mainly focuses on the development, modification and degradation of new Mg based biomaterials in vivo and in vitro. Its potential applications include hard tissue replacement and repair, degradation intracavitary stem and tissue engineering scaffolds.

spinel ceramics:It is a kind of magnesia porcelain with face-centered cubic structure. Its dielectric loss is lower than that of steatite porcelain, its dielectric constant is slightly higher, its chemical stability is good, and the hot dilute acid or cold concentrated acid has no erosion phenomenon after a long time of action, even HF acid or molten caustic soda has no significant effect on it. In the radio industry, magnesium aluminum spinel porcelain can be used to produce low voltage and high frequency capacitors, induction coil frameworks and electronic tube sockets.

Mg Mn ferrite:A spinel structure ferrite with rectangular magnetic hysteresis is commonly used as a moment magnetic material. Mainly used as a memory core for various types of electronic computers, it has many applications in automatic control, radar navigation, space navigation, information display, and other fields. In recent years, although there are many kinds of new memories, magnetic memories (especially magnetic core memories) still play an important role in computing technology due to the rich raw materials, simple process, stable performance and low cost of ferrite magnetic materials.

magnox alloy:A magnesium based alloy that resists oxidation and reaction between metals. The name originates from the oxidation resistant alloy formed by adding a small amount of calcium or beryllium to magnesium, which means oxidation resistant magnesium, namely magnesium no oxidation. Magnox AL80 (A12) is widely used, which is a alloy formed by adding 0.80% (mass fraction) of aluminum and 0.01% of beryllium to magnesium. The alloy formed by adding 0.55% Zr or 0.70% Mn to magnesium is called Magnox ZR55 (ZA) and Magnox MN70 respectively. The strength of magnesium Knox ZR55 is higher near room temperature and lower than that of magnesium Knox AL80 at temperatures above 400 ° C. When Magnox ZR55 is used as cladding materials, an intermediate layer (aluminum or carbon) should be added between the cladding and the natural uranium core to prevent plutonium from diffusion outward through the cladding. Magnox AL80 and Magnox ZR55 are mainly used for the production of natural uranium fuel, graphite moderator and carbon dioxide coolant, fuel element cladding and end plug of dual purpose power reactor (Magnox type reactor), etc. In order to increase heat exchange efficiency, the cladding is often made into herringbone or multi head spiral heat dissipation fins. Magnox MN70 is mainly used as structural materials of fuel element except cladding due to its high strength and good creep resistance.

magnesia carbon brick:Refractory products made of magnesia and graphite as main raw materials (carbon content is generally 8% -20%) have low apparent porosity, excellent slag corrosion resistance, slag penetration resistance, thermal shock resistance and thermal conductivity. It is mainly used as lining for steelmaking converter, electric arc furnace and ladle.

magnesium alloy sacrificial anodes:In electrochemical cathodic protection technology, it is a sacrificial anode materials that provides cathodic current for the protected metal to realize cathodic protection and continuously dissolves. Magnesium alloy sacrificial anodes has the characteristics of large theoretical capacitance (2.2A_h/g), negative potential, low current efficiency (about 50%), and is suitable for the protection of metal components in soil and fresh water with high resistivity, including three series of high purity magnesium, Mg Mn, and Mg Al Zn Mn, which are usually prepared by foundry and extrusion. The magnesium anode can be made into different shapes and sizes according to the conditions of the use environment and the different objects to be protected. For example, for the protection of buried oil/water tanks, foundry D-shaped or trapezoidal bar shaped anodes are commonly used; Extrusion ribbon anode is often used to protect pipelines in high resistivity soil; For the protection of domestic water heater tank, extrusion or foundry round rod anode is often used; For household water heaters where the electric heating rod is placed in the water tank, the main function of the anode is to suppress the formation of scale on the electric heating rod.

magnesium type ignition composition:Ignition composition with magnesium powder as the main combustible. Due to the low price and wide source of magnesium, magnesium series ignition propellant is widely used to ignite illuminating composition, tracer, incendiary composition, bait, signal agent and propellant with high ignition point. Magnesium is a kind of active metal, which can react with boiling water to release hydrogen, and will produce spontaneous combustion and spontaneous explosion when exposed to moisture. The ignition point of the powder cloud is 600 ° C, and the electrostatic sensitivity is 120mJ; Magnesium is easy to combustion. The heat of combustion is 2512J/g. Magnesium type ignition composition mainly include ignition composition consisting of 50% -70% magnesium, 50% -30% polylctrafluorocthylone, ignition composition consisting of 10% magnesium, 75% potassium nitrate, 15% phenolic resin, and ignition composition using magnesium and cerium alloys as combustible agents, such as ignition composition consisting of 40% cerium magnesium alloy (80/20) and 60% lead dioxide.

magnesium smelting process:There are two kinds: chlorinated molten salt electrolysis smelting process technology and silicothermic reduction process technology. The chloride molten salt electrolysis method includes two major processes: the production of magnesium chloride and the electrolysis of magnesium. The commonly used thermal reduction method in the magnesium industry is the Pijiang method, which is also a widely used method in China. The Pidgeon process for magnesium smelting is to grind calcined dolomite and ferrosilicon into fine powder according to a certain proportion, press them into briquettes, put them into a distiller made of heat resistant alloy, reduce them under the conditions of 1423-1473K and 13Pa to obtain magnesium vapor, crystallize them into solid magnesium by condensation, and then melt them into magnesium ingots. Pidgeon process has low investment and high product quality. The quality grade of magnesium produced by smelting is slightly higher than that of electrolytic magnesium.

magnesia porcelain:The ceramics with MgO containing key silicate as the main crystal phase have two colors: beige interior and ivory white. According to the main crystal phase, it can be divided into protoenstatite ceramics, forsterite porcelain, spinel porcelain and cordierite porcelain. Porcelain is delicate and creamy white, with a thin translucent body and a glossy fabric. High frequency insulating electric porcelain, high-grade household porcelain, etc.

Mooney scorch:At a given temperature, the time required to measure the Mooney viscosity of the rubber compound with a large rotor Mooney viscosity meter when it drops to the minimum value and then rises by 5 more Mooney values is called the Mooney scorch time. For a small rotor Mooney viscosity meter, the Mooney scorch time is the time required to rise by 3 more Mooney values after dropping to the minimum value. It can be used as an evaluation of the initial vulcanization characteristics of rubber compound. The test temperature is generally 120 ° C, 125 ° C or 145 ° C. The length of Mooney scorching time reflects the resistance to scorching of the rubber compound, so the processing safety of the rubber compound can be obtained.

Mooney viscosity:Also called rotational (Mooney) viscosity. The resistance shear torque value of crude rubber or rubber mixture to the rotor measured with a Mooney viscosity meter, and the shear torque of 0.083N. m is specified as one Mooney viscosity value. The measurement of Mooney viscosity value is to put the sample into the Mooney viscosity meter cavity, close the mold and preheat it for a certain time (standard is 1min), and then the rotor applies a certain shear torque to the sample. At the same time, the rotor is also subjected to the rubber resistance shear torque, and its torque is indicated by the precision load sensor, which is the Mooney viscosity value. Mooney viscosity time curve can also show the vulcanization process performance of rubber compound. Since American M. Mooney invented the Mooney viscosity meter in 1934, Mooney viscosity has become an important performance index of synthetic rubber. In the rubber industry, Mooney viscosity is commonly used to measure the plasticity of rubber to evaluate the difference between its processing properties and physical and mechanical properties, and to control the processing properties of rubber compound. The rubber with low Mooney viscosity is not easy to mixing, and it is easy to roll, so the adhesive force of the glue is low, and the physical and mechanical properties of the finished product are low; If the Mooney viscosity is too high, the plastic property of the rubber is low, it is difficult to mixing evenly and extrude, the shrinkage is large, the rolling is easy to peel off, the pattern edges and corners of the molded products are unclear, and the surface of the products is rough.

motan wax:It is also called lignite wax. It is a kind of mineral wax containing wax, resin and pitch extracted from waxy lignite by organic solvent (benzene, toluene, gasoline, etc.). The wax content of raw material coal shall be more than 3%, the particle size shall be less than 4mm, and the moisture content shall be less than 20%. Lignite wax can be used as a substitute and supplement for expensive natural animal wax and vegetable wax to produce carbon paper, shoe polish, floor wax, polishing wax, metal polish, wire and cable, leather decoration, releasing agent, etc. It is also used as wax mold for precision casting, additive for rubber and road pitch.

montmorillonite:It is also called jiaoling stone and microcrystalline kaolinite. Layered silicate mineral. A clump like aggregate that appears fluffy under an electron microscope. White, containing impurities dyed yellow, rose red, and blue-green. Earthy luster, slightly greasy luster. Smooth feeling. Add water to expand, increase the volume several times and turn into a paste. The volume shrinks after being heated and dehydrated. It is mainly produced in the weathering crust of the basic volcanic rock, and can also be formed by the decomposition of volcanic ash deposited on the sea floor. Montmorillonite has strong cation exchange ability and strong adsorption, and can be used as bleaching agent, adsorbent, filler, etc.

manganese white copper:The alloy is made of copper nickel alloy and manganese. Manganese white copper contains 2% -44% Ni, 0.1% -28% Mn, and the rest is copper. When the content of manganese in alloy is high, it can be strengthened by the precipitation of compound MnNi phase, and become a heat treatment strengthened alloy. Manganese can improve the strength, elasticity, corrosion resistance and resistivity of white copper, reduce the adverse effect of excess carbon in white copper, and improve the processability of alloy. In the range of 0-lOO ℃, the temperature coefficient of resistance is very small, and the resistance value is stable. It has a moderate resistance coefficient and low thermal potential to copper, and is mainly used as a precision resistor for standard resistors, bridges, potential difference meters, and other resistance components for instruments and meters. Cu-20Ni-1.5Mn alloy (constantan) has low resistance temperature coefficient and can be used in a wide temperature range. Its heat resistance and corrosion resistance are better than Cu-12Mn-3Ni alloy, and it has good machinability. However, for copper with large thermal potential, it is only suitable for AC precision resistance, sliding resistance, starting or regulating transformer, resistance strain gauge, etc., as well as thermocouple and thermocouple compensation wire materials. Cu-40Ni-0.5Mn (copper) has high thermoelectric potential and good linearity of thermoelectric potential temperature curve, so it is used as negative materials of thermocouple.

ferrosilicomanganese:Also known as ferrosilicomanganese, iron, manganese and silicon composition ferroalloy is used as deoxidizer or alloy element additive, with the usual manganese content of 62% -68%, silicon content of 16% -27%, and carbon content of 0.5% -2.5%. Ferrosilicomanganese is produced by continuous smelting of manganese oxide and silicon dioxide in manganese ore (including manganese rich slag) and silicon ore simultaneously reduced by carbon in submerged arc furnace. Ferrosilicomanganese is a commonly used composition deoxidizer and alloy additive for steelmaking, as well as the main raw material for the production of medium and low carbon ferromanganese and the reducing agent for the production of metallic manganese by silicothermic method. GB/T 4008-2008 has clear regulations on the grades and chemical composition of manganese silicon alloys, and each grade is divided into three levels based on the content of impurity element phosphorus.

manganese brass:Brass with manganese added on the basis of copper zinc alloy. Manganese mainly plays a solid solution strengthening role, can improve the strength and hardness of brass, and significantly improve the corrosion resistance of brass in seawater, chloride and overheating steam. Manganese brass can withstand hot and cold pressure processing, and its manganese content is usually 1% -4%. 58Cu-40Zn-2Mn alloy has good hot and cold working properties. The soft tensile strength is 400MPa, and the elongation is 40%. It is suitable for making wear-resistant piston heads, valve stems, guide rods, etc. It is widely used in the shipbuilding industry for corrosion resistant structural parts. 57Cu-39Zn-3Mn-lAl, 55Cu-41Zn-3Mn-lFe and other alloy are manganese brass with a small amount of aluminum, iron and other elements added respectively. They have high strength, soft tensile strength of 500-55OMPa, elongation of 15% -25%, and can withstand hot pressure processing. They are widely used in shipbuilding and military industries.

manganese nodule:Also called polymetallic nodules. An irregular nucleation body rich in iron and manganese minerals. It is spherical, round, potato, grape, flat, slag, etc. Sizes range from a few micrometers to tens of centimeters. The color is black and brownish black, with a layered structure. Manganese minerals are mainly hydroxymanganite, containing quartze, zeolite, plagioclase, caldte and other clastic minerals. Mainly distributed on the ocean floor plains with a depth of 4000-6000m. Rich in manganese (27% -30%), nickel (1.25% -1.5%), copper (1% -1.4%), and cobalt (0.2% -0.25%), it is a promising seabed solid mineral with great development prospects.

manganese bronze:Copper alloy with Meng as the main alloy element. The commonly used manganese bronze in industry includes Cu-1.5Mn and Cu-5Mn alloy. Copper manganese alloy has good mechanical properties, heat resistance, corrosion resistance, and can be used for cold and hot processing. In industry, Meng bronze is used to make high-temperature working parts, electronic instrument parts, steam boiler pipe fittings and various joints, steam pipes, steam valves, spark plugs, and various welding parts of boilers. Multi element copper manganese alloy, such as Cu-35Mn-2Ni high-temperature solder, Cu-35Mn-14Zn elasticity alloy, Cu-20Ni-20Mn new elasticity materials, these alloy belong to heat treatment strengthening type. The tensile strength of the latter can reach 1274MPa, and the elastic modulus E can reach 14700OMPa. Cu-42Mn-2Al is a damping alloy with good damping performance, which has been used to manufacture damping parts in industry.

bioinert composite:It refers to a kind of biomedical composite materials that can maintain stability in the biological environment without or only with weak chemical reaction. Since there is no completely inert materials in practice, bioinert materials are basically free of chemical reaction in the body.

bioerosion:Due to the biological activity of organisms, the properties of non living substances undergo changes that are not conducive to human needs, that is, the intrinsic value of non living substances is weakened. This change can be mechanical, physical, chemical, or aesthetic, not necessarily the chemical decomposition of matter. The bioerosion process can be divided into two categories: ① mechanical, including non nutritive substances eroded and perforated by insects and rodents, such as insulation and lead pipes on cables damaged; Buildings or other structures can also suffer damage due to the growth of plant roots; ② Chemical, including assimilation and alienation effects, assimilation effect refers to the use of substrates in substances by organisms as nutrient sources; Alienation effect refers to the biological production of metabolites, such as acids or toxins, which can cause corrosion, mildew, discoloration, deterioration or render them unusable.

biological engineering titanium alloy:Titanium alloy suitable for implantation on human organs. As a bioengineering materials, pure titanium has three outstanding advantages: first, it has strong corrosion resistance in human tissues; Second, it has good compatibility with human cell tissues and does not cause allergic reaction; Third, it not only has higher strength to ensure the safety factor of artificial organ, but also has lower elastic modulus to reduce the stress shielding effect of bone on the implant. First, it is used to manufacture artificial organ with less stress, such as pacemakers, artificial joint and demure holders.

biochemical sedimentary rocks:It is a rock formed by the accumulation of substances in colloidal solution and true solution in the weathering products of rock through biochemical action or biological physiological activities. Such rock often have biological or bioclastic structures. Common biochemigenic rock include diatomite, shell limestone, reef limestone, phosphorite, etc.

bioactivity:Materials have the ability of living tissue or organ functions, such as detoxification of liver, filtration of kidney, blood pumping of heart and defense function of immune system, that is, internal and external substances can cause various biological effects after acting on organisms. Biological materials that cause special biological or chemical reactions on the surface of materials, promote or affect the connection between tissues and materials, induce cell activity or regenerate new tissues are called bioactive materials.

bioactive glass:Bioactive glass is a special type of glass, which has the ability to interact with biological tissues and is usually used in medical and biomedical applications. This glass can promote bone repair and regeneration, because it has biocompatibility, can combine with bone tissue, and stimulate specific biological response in vivo. Applications of bioactive glass include orthopedic implantation, dental restorative materials and drug delivery systems.

bioactive glass-ceramics:Also known as bioactive crystallite glass. It is a glass-ceramics that contains apatite microcrystals in its composition, or can react with tissue fluid to form hydroxyapatite layer on its surface although it does not contain apatite. Its structure usually contains one or more crystalline phases. Mainly calcium metaphosphate glass-ceramics.

A-W bioactive glass-ce-ramies:Also known as A-W bioactive crystallite glass, this materials contains glass-ceramics apatite microcrystals with specific composition of two crystalline phases, namely, apatite and wollastonite, which endow it with bioactivity and enable it to form bone bonding with natural bone. Its interfacial bonding strength reaches 13MPa, showing good bioactivity. A-W bioactive glass has been used to repair bone defects and prosthesis such as artificial joint, artificial tooth root, vertebra, skull and jaw.

bioactive glass microspheres:Generally, it refers to spherical bioactive glass particles with a particle size of 50 mm to 2 mm. microsphere have obvious surface effect due to their small size, for example, materials have good affinity, high biocompatibility, and are easy to be absorbed and dispersed in organisms. The research shows that bioactive glass can quickly combine with the surrounding bone tissue after being implanted into human body, and can stimulate the activity of osteoblasts. The rougher the surface of biaoctive glass microspheres is, the more conducive to improving the adhesion of osteoblasts on them. However, due to the obvious difference in density between solid ceramics and surrounding media, it is easy to generate high shear stress, This affects the growth of cells on these ceramics microsphere.

bioactive materials:A biological materials that causes special biological or chemical reactions on the surface of materials, promotes or affects the connection between tissue and materials, cell behavior, and tissue regeneration. With the development of science, the concept of "bioactivity" has been greatly expanded. For example, some materials such as natural polymer, synthetic peptides, peptide nucleic acids, etc. can show specific bioactivity; Loading bioactivity substances through materials, and then releasing the active substances to interact with biological systems, is also considered as bioactive materials;

bioactivity composite:It is a kind of biomedical composite materials that can induce or regulate bioactivity. It is also believed that bioactivity is the property of promoting cell activity or new tissue regeneration. Some biomedical polymer, especially some natural polymer materials and synthetic macromolecule materials are regarded as bioactive materials. Hydroxyapatite is a typical bioactivity composite. Since the main inorganic component of human bone is the materials, when materials is implanted into human body, it can not only conduct bone formation, but also form bone bonding with new bone. When implanted in muscle, flexible band or subcutaneously, it can close to the tissue without inflammation or irritation reaction.

bioactivation:The technology to improve the performance of materials and endow them with the characteristics required in specific biomedical fields, that is, the purpose, types and methods of bioactivation are numerous and numerous. Taking implantation of titanium and its alloy into human body as an example, coating bioactiw ceramics layer such as hydroxyapatite on the surface of metallic materials can improve its Wood compatibility. At the same time, the preparation of active oxide layer on the surface of metallic materials can also improve the biocompatibility of materials

bioactive bone cement:Generally, it refers to a new type of calcium phosphate bone cement with good biocompatibility and similar to human hard tissue. This kind of bone cemem contains solid and liquid phases, in which the solid phase is a mixed powder of two or more calcium phosphate salts, and the liquid phase is low concentration phosphoric acid or phosphate solution or other liquids, such as plasma, citric acid solution, etc. After mixing the solid-liquid phases in proportion, they can be adjusted into any shaped paste mixture and injected into the repair site. This kind of bone cement can harden under the environment and temperature of human body, and the final composition will be converted into hydroxyapatite. The composition of this kind of bone cement is similar to that of natural bone. After being implanted into the human body, it can participate in metabolism, promote the growth of bone tissue, and eventually gradually rebuild a solid bone with load-bearing capacity. The integration of this new process and new materials has the integration of clinical use. It can be shaped arbitrarily according to the needs of the defect site, and the operation is simple. This overcomes the difficulties in preparing bone repair materials with powder and granular materials, poor mechanical properties and easy loss. At present, the research includes different types of calcium phosphate bone cemem such as p-TCP system, crTCP system and TECP system.

bioactive bonding:It refers to a series of biophysical and chemical reactions between the implanted body and the tissue (especially bone tissue) interface after the bioactive materials is implanted into the human body. At the same time, with the participation of cells, an interface bond with certain mechanical strength is formed between the tissue and the implanted body. Materials that can form bioactive bonding with surrounding tissues after being implanted into the body mainly include bioactive glass, hydroxyapatite, tricalcium phosphate, etc.

Absorbable bioceramics refer to ceramics that can be gradually degradation and absorbed in the physiological environment, and then replaced by new tissues to repair or replace damaged tissues, such as gypsum, phosphoric acid=calcium ceramics. According to composition, biological cemmics can be divided into single component biological cemmics and composition biological cemmics. One component biological cemmics consist of only one biological cemmics, such as alumina ceramics and hydroxyapatite ceramics. Composition biological cemmics refer to biological cemmics composed of two or more components, such as biphasic biological cemmics. According to its functions, biological cemmics can be divided into alternative repair biological cemmics, therapeutic biological cemmics and multi-functional biological cemmics=alternative repair biological cemmics mainly refer to traditional ceramics used to replace or repair and reconstruct human lesions or injured parts. For example, alumina ceramics are used to replace femoral joints, hydroxyapatite ceramics are used to repair defective bones, and bioglass is used to replace ossicles. Therapeutic biological cemmics mainly refer to ceramics that have targeted treatment for human diseases, such as magnetic biological cemmics, drug loaded biological cemmics, hot compress biological cemmics, etc. Multi functional biological cemmics refer to biological cemmics that have multiple functions for human body, such as piezoelectric bioceramics. On the one hand, they can repair bone defects, and on the other hand, they can repair bone defects due to their "poor" performance? Electricity can also promote the growth of bone tissue.

bioactive gradient coating:Biological cemmics are a kind of ceramics materials that can cause specific reaction of surrounding tissues or chemical bonding with tissues, such as hydroxyapatite, bioactive glass and bioactive glass-ceramics. Absorbable bioceramics mean that they can be gradually degradation and absorbed in the physiological environment, and gradually replaced by new tissues over time, so as to repair or replace damaged tissues, such as gypsum and calcium phosphate ceramics. According to its composition, biological cemmics can be divided into single component biological cemmics and composition biological cemmics. One component biological cemmics consist of a single kind of biological cemmics, such as alumina ceramics and hydroxyapatite ceramics. Composition biological cemmics are composed of two or more components, such as dual phase biological cemmics. According to its functions, biological cemmics can be divided into substitute repair biological cemmics, therapeutic biological cemmics and multi-functional biological cemmics. Replacement repair biological cemmics are mainly used to replace or repair human lesions or damaged parts, such as alumina ceramics for hip joint replacement, hydroxyapatite ceramics for bone defect repair, bioglass for auditory ossicles replacement, etc. Therapeutic biological cemmics are mainly used for targeted treatment of human diseases, such as magnetic biological cemmics, drug loaded biological cemmics and hot compress biological cemmics. Multi functional biological cemmics refer to biological cemmics with multiple functions in the human body, such as piezoelectric bioceramics, which can not only repair the defective bone tissue, but also promote the growth of bone tissue due to its piezoelectric properties
